title: Fusarium longipes: a mycoparasite of Sclerospora graminicola on pearl millet
abstract:
A mycoparasite, Fusor~urn longipes Wollenw. and Reinking, parasitizing Sclerospora graminicola,
that causes downy mildew of pearl 'millet is reported. The mycoparasite attacks only downy mildew-infected
areas of the leaves. The mycoparasite produces brownish spots with variable sizes and may cover the entire
infected leaf under humid conditions resulting in the complete inhibition of sporangial produc~ion. Such plants
are killed immaturely. Attempts to selectively control the mycoparasite by the use of fungicide or to identify
genotypes which can support S. graminicola but not F. longipes have been unsuccessful
